name: Kalamarakia Pilafi (Squid Baked with Rice)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and clean the squid, separating the outer sacs from the heads and tentacles. Remove and discard the translucent cartilage, small sand bag, and ink.
2. Rub salt on the outer sacs and rinse them inside and out with cold water. Rinse the head and tentacles thoroughly as well.
3. Slice the squid into uniform rings, between 1/2 and 1 inch wide.
4. Heat oil in a frying pan and add the squid and garlic. Sauté for 5 minutes.
5. Stir in the wine and sliced tomatoes. Cover and simmer until the squid is almost tender (approximately 30 minutes).
6. Transfer to a baking dish.
7. Meanwhile, heat butter in another frying pan and sauté the rice without browning it until transparent, stirring constantly.
8. Add the rice to the squid and sprinkle with 1/4 cup chopped parsley, rosemary, and salt and pepper to taste.
9. Add enough hot water to cook the rice (slightly more than 2 cups including the tomato sauce).
10. Cover and bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 30 to 40 minutes, or until the rice is tender.
11. Sprinkle with additional chopped parsley and serve hot.
